ucs.title: [LLM] EXOTIC LILY: WeTransfer/TransferNow lure delivering ISO→LNK→BumbleBee loader chain
ucs.description: Hunts EXOTIC LILY's signature initial-access tradecraft: a spear-phishing email pointing to a legitimate file-sharing service (WeTransfer, TransferNow, OneDrive, Disk.Yandex) that delivers an ISO/IMG container, which is mounted to execute an LNK that side-loads the BumbleBee DLL via rundll32 — the documented hand-off to Conti/Diavol affiliates (Wizard Spider / TrickBot cluster).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Email where (Email.url IN ("*wetransfer.com*","*we.tl/*","*transfernow.net*","*disk.yandex*","*1drv.ms*","*onedrive.live.com*","*mediafire.com*","*sendspace.com*")) by Email.recipient Email.src_user Email.subject Email.url Email.message_id _time | rename Email.recipient as user Email.src_user as sender Email.url as lure_url | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Web where Web.url IN ("*wetransfer.com*","*we.tl/*","*transfernow.net*","*disk.yandex*") by Web.user Web.dest Web.url _time | rename Web.user as user] | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk") by Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.dest _time | rename Filesystem.user as user Filesystem.dest as host] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe") (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="odbcconf.exe" OR Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe") (Processes.process="*.dll*" AND (Processes.process="*,*" OR Processes.process="*/s*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process _time | rename Processes.dest as host] | stats count min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(lure_url) as lure_url values(sender) as sender values(file_name) as file values(process) as bumblebee_cmdline by user host | where count>=1
ucs.kql:
let lure_hosts = dynamic(["wetransfer.com","we.tl","transfernow.net","disk.yandex.com","1drv.ms","onedrive.live.com","mediafire.com","sendspace.com"]);
let phish_clicks = UrlClickEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where Url has_any (lure_hosts)
  | project ClickTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, Url, NetworkMessageId, ActionType;
let phish_email = EmailUrlInfo
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where Url has_any (lure_hosts)
  | join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | project NetworkMessageId, SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, EmailDirection)
      on NetworkMessageId
  | project EmailTime=Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, SenderFromAddress, SenderMailFromDomain, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, LureUrl=Url;
let iso_drops = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img" or FileName endswith ".lnk"
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe")
  | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountUpn, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256;
let bumblebee_exec = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","odbcconf.exe","regsvr32.exe")
  | where ProcessCommandLine has ".dll" and (ProcessCommandLine has "," or ProcessCommandLine has " /s " or ProcessCommandLine has "-a -f")
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","powershell.exe")
  | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\Device\\CdRom|\\\?\\Volume|[A-Z]:\\[^\\]+\.dll"
  | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountUpn, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
phish_email
| join kind=inner phish_clicks on $left.RecipientEmailAddress == $right.AccountUpn
| join kind=inner (iso_drops) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.InitiatingProcessAccountUpn
| join kind=inner bumblebee_exec on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + 1h) and ExecTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m)
| project EmailTime, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, LureUrl, DeviceName, FileName1=FileName, ISO=FileName, BumblebeeCmd=ProcessCommandLine, SHA256
ucs.rationale: Google TAG's March 2022 EXOTIC LILY report and subsequent Mandiant/Microsoft reporting documented this exact chain: spoofed-vendor email → file-sharing link (most often WeTransfer/TransferNow) → ISO containing LNK + hidden DLL → rundll32 launches BumbleBee, which is the loader handed to Wizard Spider/Conti for ransomware staging. Joining the four pivots (lure-domain in email + click + ISO/LNK drop

ucs.title: [LLM] EXOTIC LILY: typosquatted vendor-spoofing sender with attached/linked container payload
ucs.description: Hunts EXOTIC LILY's reconnaissance-and-resource-development tradecraft (T1583.001/T1585/T1597) where they register lookalike domains of legitimate companies (often appending '-co', '-us', '.us', '.co' or character swaps) and impersonate real employees they enumerate from LinkedIn/RocketReach to send file-sharing lures to the victim's business contacts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Email where Email.message_id=* by Email.src_user Email.recipient Email.subject Email.message_id _time | rex field=Email.src_user "@(?<sender_domain>[^>]+)" | eval sender_root=replace(sender_domain,"\.(com|net|org|io|co|us|biz)$","") | join type=inner [| inputlookup corp_partner_domains.csv | rename domain as legit_domain | eval legit_root=replace(legit_domain,"\.(com|net|org|io|co|us|biz)$","")] | eval lev=if(sender_root!=legit_root AND (like(sender_root, legit_root."%") OR like(sender_root, "%".legit_root) OR len(sender_root)=len(legit_root)),1,0) | where lev=1 AND sender_domain!=legit_domain | search Email.url IN ("*wetransfer.com*","*transfernow.net*","*we.tl/*","*disk.yandex*","*1drv.ms*") OR Email.file_name IN ("*.iso","*.img","*.lnk","*.iso.zip") | stats count values(Email.subject) as subjects values(Email.recipient) as recipients values(sender_domain) as spoof_domain values(legit_domain) as impersonated by Email.src_user | where count>=1
ucs.kql:
let known_partners = externaldata(domain:string) [@"https://internal/lookups/partner_domains.csv"] with (format="csv", ignoreFirstRecord=true);
let partner_roots = known_partners | extend root = tostring(split(domain, ".")[0]) | summarize by root, domain;
EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
| extend SenderDomain = tolower(SenderMailFromDomain)
| extend SenderRoot = tostring(split(SenderDomain, ".")[0])
| join kind=inner partner_roots on $left.SenderRoot == $right.root
| where SenderDomain != domain
| join kind=inner (EmailUrlInfo | where Url has_any ("wetransfer.com","we.tl","transfernow.net","disk.yandex","1drv.ms","onedrive.live.com")) on NetworkMessageId
| join kind=leftouter (EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img" or FileName endswith ".lnk" or FileName endswith ".iso.zip") on NetworkMessageId
| project Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, SenderDomain, ImpersonatedDomain=domain, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, Url, FileName, SHA256
| extend Severity = iff(isnotempty(FileName) or Url has_any ("wetransfer.com","transfernow.net"), "High", "Medium")
ucs.rationale: EXOTIC LILY's distinguishing pre-attack TTP is buying lookalike domains of the victim's real business partners (Google TAG noted ~5,000 emails/day at peak from these typosquatted domains) and pairing them with file-sharing links. Joining typosquat detection against an internal partner-domain lookup with a file-share URL or ISO/LNK attachment in the same message yields a much higher-fidelity signal
